display.purpose: Page-level and section headings. Used sparingly — one per major region.

headline.purpose: Sub-section headings. Lighter weight than Display to create hierarchy.

title.purpose: Card titles, form section labels, dialog headings.

body.purpose: All body copy, form hints, descriptive text. Max 70ch line length.

label.purpose: Status badges, case tags, monospace identifiers. Uppercase, tracked. Never body copy.

shadows.name: stacked-paper
shadows.purpose: Applied to interactive elements at rest (buttons, clickable cards). Collapses to none on :active as the element translates (2px, 2px). No blur. No other shadow is used in this system.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: The only animation in this system. On :active, buttons and clickable cards translate(2px, 2px) and their shadow collapses. Instant feedback, short duration.

motion.name: reduced-motion-press
motion.purpose: At prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, the press animation is suppressed. The shadow collapses without the translate — state changes but the motion stops.

breakpoints.name: demo-min
breakpoints.purpose: Minimum projector-safe viewport. Layout must be readable and non-overflowing at this width.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The primary action in any form or dialog. Styled as a stamped document control.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Fredoka', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #4a3f35; background: #a0c4ff; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 #4a3f35; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.1s ease, box-shadow 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(0.95); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #a0c4ff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translate(2px, 2px); box-shadow: none; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { cursor: not-allowed; opacity: 0.5; box-shadow: none; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-primary { transition: none; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: none; } }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action — used alongside a primary, never as the sole CTA.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { font-family: 'Fredoka', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #4a3f35; background: #bdb2ff; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 #4a3f35; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.1s ease, box-shadow 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { filter: brightness(0.95); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #a0c4ff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: translate(2px, 2px); box-shadow: none; } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { cursor: not-allowed; opacity: 0.5; box-shadow: none; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-btn-secondary { transition: none; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: none; } }

components.name: Status Badge — Available
components.description: Official status stamp for animals cleared for adoption. Cleared Mint fill.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #4a3f35; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; border-radius: 4px; padding: 0.25rem 0.5rem; } .ds-badge--available { background: #caffbf; } .ds-badge--critical { background: #ffadad; } .ds-badge--pending { background: #ffd6a5; } .ds-badge--info { background: #9bf6ff; } .ds-badge--celebration { background: #ffc6ff; } .ds-badge--tag { background: #fdffb6; }

components.name: Case-File Card (Clickable)
components.description: A case-file document that acts as a navigation surface. Real button semantics, stacked-paper shadow.
components.css: .ds-case-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.25rem; text-align: left; font-family: 'Karla', sans-serif; color: #4a3f35; background: #fffffc; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 #4a3f35; padding: 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.1s ease, box-shadow 0.1s ease; } .ds-case-card:hover { filter: brightness(0.97); } .ds-case-card:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #a0c4ff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-case-card:active { transform: translate(2px, 2px); box-shadow: none; } .ds-case-card__title { font-family: 'Fredoka', sans-serif; font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-case-card__subtitle { font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-case-card__description { font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.5; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-case-card { transition: none; } .ds-case-card:active { transform: none; } }

components.name: Form Field
components.description: Native input with label, optional hint, and optional error state. Linked via aria-describedby.
components.html: Used for adoption follow-up only.
components.css: .ds-form-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.25rem; } .ds-form-field__label { font-family: 'Karla', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #4a3f35; } .ds-form-field__input { font-family: 'Karla', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #4a3f35; background: #fffffc; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; width: 100%; } .ds-form-field__input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #a0c4ff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-form-field__hint { font-family: 'Karla', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #6b5e52; } .ds-form-field__error { font-family: 'Karla', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #8a1010; }

components.name: Chat Bubble — Concierge
components.description: The AI Concierge's streaming response. Left-aligned, bordered, aria-live on the container.
components.html: Understood. Reviewing the current roster for low-maintenance placements.
components.css: .ds-chat-region { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-chat-bubble { font-family: 'Karla', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #4a3f35; max-width: 70%; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; border-radius: 8px; } .ds-chat-bubble--concierge { background: #fffffc; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; align-self: flex-start; } .ds-chat-bubble--user { background: #a0c4ff; border: 2px solid #4a3f35; align-self: flex-end; }

narrative.overview:
A filing-office worker with a surprisingly cheerful desk. The metaphor resolves every design question: the clerk half answers "should this feel official?" (yes, always); the candy-colored half answers "is this too cold or sterile?" (never). The design system is a municipal bureaucracy that happens to be outfitted in periwinkle and mint — not a playful app that happens to have a serious subject.

Every surface is flat and form-like. Borders are inked, not blurred. Status is stamped, not implied by subtle shade. The display font (Fredoka, rounded and friendly) gives the clerk a warm personality; the monospace stamps (Space Mono) give the paperwork its official register.

This system explicitly rejects the SaaS-cream aesthetic and the playful-startup aesthetic. The candy colors are status indicators — not decoration, and never ironic.
keyCharacteristics: Flat fills only — no gradients between any two palette colors, ever
Stacked-paper shadow (2px solid offset, no blur) as the only depth signal
Corner radius never exceeds 8px — gently curved, not bubbly
2px solid ink borders on every interactive element
Projector-legible: all body and interactive text ≥16px, high contrast on near-white

rules.name: The Status Lock Rule
rules.body: Each status color belongs to exactly one state. Cleared Mint means 'available.' Alert Salmon means 'critical.' Pending Peach means 'in treatment.' Roster Cyan means 'informational.' Adoption Pink means 'adopted.' None may be used decoratively.

rules.name: The No-Gradient Rule
rules.body: No gradient between any two palette colors. Not between Intake Periwinkle and Treatment Lavender. Not between Cleared Mint and Form White. Flat fills only. A gradient in this system is not a design choice — it is an error.

rules.name: The Font-Crossing Rule
rules.body: Fredoka is for display and headings. Karla is for body. Space Mono is for badges and labels. They do not cross.

rules.name: The Scale Floor Rule
rules.body: On this projector-facing demo, 1rem is the minimum for any text the audience is expected to read. Badge text (0.75rem in Space Mono) is the only exception.

rules.name: The One Shadow Rule
rules.body: One shadow, one role, one behavior. Never add blur. Never vary the offset per component. Never use box-shadow as a decorative accent.

rules.name: The Press Rule
rules.body: On :active, interactive elements translate (2px, 2px) and their shadow collapses. This must be consistent across every button and clickable card.

dos: Do use flat fills. Every color is a flat fill. Gradients between palette colors are prohibited.
Do keep corner radius at or below 8px. Anything rounder is the playful-startup aesthetic this system rejects.
Do use the stacked-paper shadow (2px 2px 0 0 #4a3f35) for interactive elements at rest — and collapse it on :active with a translate(2px, 2px).
Do use Space Mono for badges, stamps, and case identifiers — uppercase, tracked.
Do use native semantic HTML: <button>, <input type='checkbox'>, <label for='...'>, <dialog>.
Do write every label, error message, and empty state in complete bureaucratic sincerity.
Do keep all interactive and body text at or above 1rem (16px) — projector legibility.
Do use aria-live='polite' on the streaming chat container.
donts: Don't use gradients — between palette colors, between a palette color and transparent, or as a decorative background.
Don't use soft drop-shadows (blurred box-shadow). One flat offset shadow, or none.
Don't use border-radius above 8px on cards, inputs, or buttons.
Don't use Adoption Pink (#ffc6ff) outside of a successful adoption moment.
Don't use Case Tag Yellow (#fdffb6) for large fills — badges only.
Don't use Inter. The specs prohibit it by name.
Don't use status colors as decorative fills.
Don't use a <div> with a click handler for interactive elements.
Don't write copy that acknowledges the joke — the UI is deadpan.
Don't introduce the SaaS-cream aesthetic: off-white body backgrounds, Inter, soft-shadow cards with no borders.
Don't introduce the playful-startup aesthetic: pastel gradient panels, consumer-app microinteractions, gamified progress indicators.
